searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover.new( options: table?-- Optional initial config overrides, see Hover.Configure ) → Hover Creates a new Hover instance. Does not start listening for hovers — call Hover:Start to begin.

searchDocs.title: Configure​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover:Configure( options: table-- A table of config keys to override ) → () Overrides one or more values in Hover.Config. Valid keys are HoverRange, ContinuousHover, and Tag. Unknown keys produce a warning and are ignored.

searchDocs.title: GetHovered​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover:GetHovered() → Instance? -- The currently hovered instance, if any Returns the instance currently being hovered, or nil if nothing is hovered.

searchDocs.title: Init​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover:Init() → () Sets up tag observation so tagged instances become hoverable. Called automatically by Hover:Start if not already initialized — you generally don't need to call this yourself.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover:Start() → () Begins listening for mouse/touch/gamepad hovers over tagged instances. Calls Hover:Init automatically if it hasn't run yet.

searchDocs.title: Stop​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover:Stop() → () Stops listening for hovers and clears any current hover state, firingHover.HoveringEnded if something was being hovered.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Hover:Destroy() → () Stops the instance, disconnects all internal listeners, and destroys its signals. The Hover instance should not be used after calling this.
